task_completion.description: Load the 4AKE structure into a molecular visualization tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL or similar software is open, displaying a 3D protein structure, with '4AKE' visible in the object list, command history, or window title.
The 3D structure in the viewer is colored using a color spectrum/gradient (typically blue for rigid/low B-factor, red for flexible/high B-factor) indicating temperature factors.

task_completion.description: Save the generated figure to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing 'adk_bfactor.png' being saved or existing in the '~/PyMOL_Data/images/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write a report listing the top 5 most flexible residues, their B-factors, and their structural domain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the report contents. It must list exactly 5 residues (which should have residue numbers between 118 and 167), their corresponding B-factor values, and identify the structural domain (e.g., 'LID domain').

task_completion.description: Save the report to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing 'adk_flexibility_report.txt' being saved or existing in the '~/PyMOL_Data/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a genuine molecular visualization tool (like PyMOL) to perform the analysis and generate the figure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the PyMOL GUI or command line to render the image, rather than downloading a pre-made image from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent extracted B-factor data from the structure rather than hallucinating the values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using commands or scripts to list, sort, or iterate over the B-factors of the residues to find the top 5.

integrity.description: The agent saved the files exactly where requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exact paths '~/PyMOL_Data/images/adk_bfactor.png' and '~/PyMOL_Data/adk_flexibility_report.txt' are explicitly visible in save dialogs, terminal commands, or file managers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: PDB ID: 4AKE. The most flexible residues correctly fall within the expected LID domain, which corresponds to residue numbers 118 through 167. The figure must be saved to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/adk_bfactor.png (minimum 30KB) and the report to ~/PyMOL_Data/adk_flexibility_report.txt (minimum 5 lines).
